description: A logical analysis tool that applies the principle of parsimony (Occam's Razor) to evaluate competing hypotheses. Capable of operating in Forensic (probabilistic, evidentiary) and Academic (theoretical, ontological) modes.

scenario_description.description: The problem, event, or phenomenon requiring explanation.

hypotheses.description: A list of competing explanations or theories to be evaluated.

mode.description: The operational environment. FORENSIC prioritizes evidence probability and likelihood. ACADEMIC prioritizes ontological economy and theoretical elegance.

constraints.description: Optional: Known facts or axioms that must be adhered to.
